name: Validation/validate-modes
description: $validate conformance tests covering the 'profile' parameter (via query string and via an embedded Parameters parameter), a resourceType mismatch between URL and body, a missing required element on a non-Patient resource, instance-level $validate by id (found and missing), and an unresolvable profile reference (from ValidateTests). Profile-enforcement assertions are warningOnly since custom StructureDefinition-based profile validation support varies across servers.

variable.name: profileSdId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the custom validation profile StructureDefinition created for this suite

variable.name: conformantPatientId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the Patient that conforms to the custom profile (has birthDate)

variable.name: nonconformantPatientId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the Patient that does not conform to the custom profile (missing birthDate)

operation.description: Create a custom StructureDefinition profile requiring Patient.birthDate (1..1)
Create a profile-conformant Patient (has birthDate) for the GET-by-id tests
Create a profile-non-conformant Patient (no birthDate) for the GET-by-id tests

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-conformant resource accepted (profile via query parameter)
test.description: POST Patient/$validate?profile=<url> with a conformant Patient (has birthDate) as a raw body; the OperationOutcome must have no error/fatal issues.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate?profile=... with a conformant Patient (raw body)

assert.description: Operation should return a 2xx success status (warningOnly: some servers use 422 for validation failures)
Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Conformant resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-conformant resource accepted (profile via Parameters parameter)
test.description: POST Patient/$validate with 'profile' and 'resource' both embedded in a Parameters body; a conformant Patient must produce no error/fatal issues.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with profile + conformant resource both embedded in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Conformant resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-non-conformant resource rejected (profile via query parameter)
test.description: POST Patient/$validate?profile=<url> with a Patient missing birthDate; the OperationOutcome should flag an error/fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers).

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate?profile=... with a non-conformant Patient (raw body, no birthDate)

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Non-conformant resource: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers)

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-non-conformant resource rejected (profile via Parameters parameter)
test.description: POST Patient/$validate with 'profile' and a non-conformant Patient (missing birthDate) both embedded in Parameters; should be flagged (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers).

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with profile + non-conformant resource both embedded in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Non-conformant resource: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers)

test.name: type-level $validate: resourceType in the URL must match resourceType in the body
test.description: POST Observation/$validate with a Patient resource body; the resourceType mismatch between URL and body must produce an error/fatal issue (from ValidateTests).

operation.description: POST to Observation/$validate with a Patient resource embedded in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Type mismatch: OperationOutcome must contain at least one error or fatal issue
An issue should reference the type mismatch via a 'TypeName' expression (warningOnly: exact expression/message format varies across servers)

test.name: type-level $validate: required element missing on a non-Patient resource (Observation.status)
test.description: POST Observation/$validate with an Observation missing the required status element; the OperationOutcome must report an error/fatal issue for the missing element (from ValidateTests).

operation.description: POST to Observation/$validate with an Observation that omits the required status element

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Missing required element: OperationOutcome must contain at least one error or fatal issue
An issue should reference Observation.status (warningOnly: issue expression format varies across servers)

test.name: instance-level $validate (GET by id): conformant resource passes
test.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=<url> for a stored, profile-conformant Patient; must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues (from ValidateTests).

operation.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=... for the profile-conformant stored Patient

assert.description: Operation should return a 2xx success status (warningOnly: some servers use 422 for validation failures)
Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Conformant resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: instance-level $validate (GET by id): non-conformant resource is rejected
test.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=<url> for a stored Patient that violates the profile; should be flagged (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers).

operation.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=... for the profile-non-conformant stored Patient

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Non-conformant resource: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers)

test.name: instance-level $validate (GET by id): non-existent id returns 404
test.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate for an id that was never created; the server must return 404 Not Found (from ValidateTests).

operation.description: GET Patient/{missing-id}/$validate for an id that was never created

assert.description: Server must return 404 Not Found for a non-existent id

test.name: type-level $validate: malformed profile parameter value
test.description: POST Patient/$validate?profile=<unresolvable-url> with an otherwise-valid Patient; the server should return 400 Bad Request (warningOnly: some servers ignore unknown profiles instead).

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate?profile=not-a-resolvable-profile-url with a valid Patient body

assert.description: Server should return 400 Bad Request for an unresolvable profile reference (warningOnly: some servers ignore unknown profiles instead)

operation.description: Teardown: delete the custom validation profile StructureDefinition
